title: Footprint-Free Genome Editing of iPSC Using Alt-R CRISPR/Cas9
author: Jacob Marsh
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfmmjk46

steps.title: Coat 1 well of a 6-well...
steps.instruction: Coat 1 well of a 6-well plate with 1 mL of Matrigel for 01:00:00 at 37 °C

steps.title: Add 5 mL of DMEM/F12 to a 15ml conical tue
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of DMEM/F12 to a 15ml conical tue

steps.title: Thaw vial of cells in 37...
steps.instruction: Thaw vial of cells in 37 °C water bath for approximately 00:01:00

steps.title: Wipe off excess water from tube...
steps.instruction: Wipe off excess water from tube and spray with 70% Ethanol before placing vial into biosafety cabinet

steps.title: Transfer thawed cells into conical tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer thawed cells into conical tube containing 5 mL DMEM/F12 If some cells remain in vial use 1 mL DMEM/F12 to rinse vial clean and transfer into the 15ml conical

steps.title: Swirl to mix cells in the...
steps.instruction: Swirl to mix cells in the DMEM/F12 and 750 rpm, °C, 3 minutes Avoid rough mixing or suspension of cells; this helps to keep the cells clustered

steps.title: After centrifugation, aspirate off most supernatant,...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, aspirate off most supernatant, leaving a small amount in the tube. Do not try to aspirate all the way down to the pellet (may or may not visible)

steps.title: Add 4 µL of Rock Inhibitor...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of Rock Inhibitor to cell pellet and resuspend in 2 mL of mTesR1 in order to achieve a 10 micromolar (µM) solution. Gently swirl to mix Avoid rough mixing or suspension of cells; this helps to keep the cells clustered

steps.title: Aspirate Matrigel from coated 6-well plate,...
steps.instruction: Aspirate Matrigel from coated 6-well plate, and pipet cell mixture into well.

steps.title: Incuate overnight at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incuate overnight at 37 °C

steps.title: After 24:00:00 change media and allow cells to recover
steps.instruction: After 24:00:00 change media and allow cells to recover

steps.title: Once cells have reached 80-90% confluency,...
steps.instruction: Once cells have reached 80-90% confluency, split culture as single cells into 3 wells of a 6 well tissue culture plate.

steps.title: Aspirate media from well containing cells
steps.instruction: Aspirate media from well containing cells

steps.title: Wash cells with 2 mL of PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 2 mL of PBS

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Accutase per well
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Accutase per well

steps.title: Incubate cells and Accutase at 37 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate cells and Accutase at 37 °C for 00:10:00 . Gently tap plate upon removal from incubator to help dislodge cells from Matrigel

steps.title: Collect cells in 5 mL of...
steps.instruction: Collect cells in 5 mL of PBS and transfer to 15ml conical tube

steps.title: 750 rpm, °C, 3 minutes
steps.instruction: 750 rpm, °C, 3 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet in 6 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet in 6 mL mTesR1 + 5 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor and plate 2 mL into each freshly coated Matrigel well

steps.title: Coat 3 wells of a 6-well...
steps.instruction: Coat 3 wells of a 6-well plate with 1 mL of Matrigel for 01:00:00 at 37 °C

steps.title: Perform the single cell passage (Step...
steps.instruction: Perform the single cell passage (Step 12) at least two additional times prior to using cells for Nucleofection

steps.title: Perform a single cell passage approximately...
steps.instruction: Perform a single cell passage approximately 48:00:00 prior to nucleofection You will need three million cells per nucleofection and one million cells per GFP control. Therefore, three wells of a confluent of a 6-well tissue culture plate are sufficient

steps.title: Coat three wells of a 6-well...
steps.instruction: Coat three wells of a 6-well plate for nucleofection using Matrigel and incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 prior to splitting cells

steps.title: Just prior to splitting cells for...
steps.instruction: Just prior to splitting cells for nucleofection, aspirate Matrigel from coated wells and add 3 mL of DMEM/F12 + 10% FBS supplemented with 10 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor per well

steps.title: Gather the following reagents for Alt-R...
steps.instruction: Gather the following reagents for Alt-R Reactions and let them thaw on ice %s %s

steps.title: Prepare Alt-R Reactions
steps.instruction: Prepare Alt-R Reactions

steps.title: Prepare fresh Alt-R gRNA by adding 2
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh Alt-R gRNA by adding 2.5 µL of 200 micromolar (µM) Alt-R crRNA and 2.5 µL of 200 micromolar (µM) Alt-R tracrRNA in equal volumes. Heat mixture at 95 °C for 00:05:00 . Let cool to Room temperature The Alt-R gRNA should be prepared fresh before each use

steps.title: Resuspend Alt-R Cas9 and Electroporation Enhancer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend Alt-R Cas9 and Electroporation Enhancer in PBS to a final concentration of 100 micromolar (µM)

steps.title: Combine Alt-R gRNA solution and Alt-R...
steps.instruction: Combine Alt-R gRNA solution and Alt-R Cas9 + Electroporation Enhancer solution together and incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 %s %s RNP Complex stability limited to 48:00:00 at 4 °C . Do not freeze

steps.title: Split cells for nucleofection
steps.instruction: Split cells for nucleofection

steps.title: Aspirate media from cells
steps.instruction: Aspirate media from cells

steps.title: Wash each well with 2 mL of PBS and aspirate
steps.instruction: Wash each well with 2 mL of PBS and aspirate

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Accutase per well
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Accutase per well

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:10:00

steps.title: Collect cells in 5 mL of...
steps.instruction: Collect cells in 5 mL of PBS and transfer to a 15ml conical tube

steps.title: 750 rpm, °C, 3 minutes
steps.instruction: 750 rpm, °C, 3 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL of PBS in the 15ml conical tube, then dilute cells 1:10 ( 10 µL of cell suspension + 90 µL of PBS) in a 1.7ml tube

steps.title: Use 10 µL of diluted cells for cell counts
steps.instruction: Use 10 µL of diluted cells for cell counts

steps.title: Using all four corners of the...
steps.instruction: Using all four corners of the countess slide, calculate the average number of cells

steps.title: Multiply the average by 10,000 (10 4 )
steps.instruction: Multiply the average by 10,000 (10 4 )

steps.title: Multiply product from step 20
steps.instruction: Multiply product from step 20.2 by 3 to get the total number of cells [Average # of Cells x 10,000 x 3] = Total Number of Cells

steps.title: Take total number of cells calculated in step 20
steps.instruction: Take total number of cells calculated in step 20.3 and divide by three million

steps.title: Take answer from step 20
steps.instruction: Take answer from step 20.4 and divide by three to get the volume of cells necessary for nucleofection

steps.title: Transfer the desired volume of cells (calculated in step 20
steps.instruction: Transfer the desired volume of cells (calculated in step 20.5) to microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: 90 x g, °C, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: 90 x g, °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate PBS from cell pellet
steps.instruction: Aspirate PBS from cell pellet

steps.title: Make reaction mix from Lonza Kit
steps.instruction: Make reaction mix from Lonza Kit: P3 Primary Cell 4D (V4XP-3024). Each reaction requires a total of 100 µL of reaction mix. If performing more than one reaction, it is best to make a Master Mix

steps.title: Combine 82 µL of P3 Solution...
steps.instruction: Combine 82 µL of P3 Solution and 18 µL of Supplement into a 1.7ml microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Combine reaction mix from step 24...
steps.instruction: Combine reaction mix from step 24 ( 100 µL ) with previously complexed DNA from step 17

steps.title: Mix reaction mix and DNA with...
steps.instruction: Mix reaction mix and DNA with cell pellets (step 23) by pipetting up and down with p200 pipette Try to pipette as little as possible. Pipette only until mixed

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL of reaction mix...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL of reaction mix + DNA + cells to a cuvette Ensure no bubbles are in the transferred mixture of cells in the cuvette. This can interfere with the nucleofection's success

steps.title: Nucleofect with Lonza Program CA-137 in P3 Solution
steps.instruction: Nucleofect with Lonza Program CA-137 in P3 Solution

steps.title: Let cuvette and cells incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Let cuvette and cells incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Transfer cells/DNA solution to approproate pre-coated...
steps.instruction: Transfer cells/DNA solution to approproate pre-coated well containing 2 mL of DMEM/F12 + 10% FBS + 10 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight

steps.title: Continue culturing the iPSC in 1...
steps.instruction: Continue culturing the iPSC in 1 well of a 6 well plate for 5-7 days post nucleofection, changing mTesR1 daily.

steps.title: 24:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with...
steps.instruction: 24:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with 5 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor

steps.title: 48:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with 2
steps.instruction: 48:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with 2.5 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor

steps.title: 72:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with...
steps.instruction: 72:00:00 post-nucleofection - add mTesR1 with 1 micromolar (µM) Rock Inhibitor

steps.title: Continue culturing cells in mTesR1 until confluent (~5 days)
steps.instruction: Continue culturing cells in mTesR1 until confluent (~5 days)
